initiatives.title: Supported AI tools and published license costs
initiatives.description: UC Merced’s AI portal lists Zoom AI Companion, Canva Magic Studio, Microsoft Copilot, Copilot for Microsoft 365, and ChatGPT Edu status, including data-protection levels and costs such as included Zoom/Copilot access, Copilot for Microsoft 365 annual pricing, and ChatGPT Edu negotiation status.
initiatives.significance: This closes a platform-contract gap with unusually concrete public tool and cost data.

research_centers.name: AI research or coordination activity

computing_infrastructure.summary: UC Merced OIT documents MERCED as an NSF-supported 1,872-core Linux HPC cluster and Pinnacles as an NSF-MRI funded no-cost faculty cluster equipped with Intel Xeon Gold 6330 CPUs and NVIDIA Tesla A100 40GB GPUs. The page confirms A100 AI-capable GPU resources but does not disclose the number of Pinnacles GPU nodes or total GPUs.

narrative_summary: UC Merced now has concrete public platform and cost evidence. Its AI portal lists supported Zoom AI Companion, Canva Magic Studio, Microsoft Copilot, Copilot for Microsoft 365, and ChatGPT Edu, including data-protection levels, data sources, availability, and pricing notes such as included Zoom/Copilot access, Copilot for Microsoft 365 annual cost, and ChatGPT Edu negotiation status. Compute evidence is also stronger: OIT documents MERCED and Pinnacles, with Pinnacles providing no-cost faculty access to Intel Xeon Gold and NVIDIA Tesla A100 GPU resources. Exact user counts, aggregate contract spend, ChatGPT Edu purchase timing, Pinnacles GPU count, and subgroup outcome metrics remain undisclosed.

Pinnacles has public A100 GPU evidence, but exact GPU count and student subgroup outcome metrics were not disclosed.
